search_terms_used: tendinopathy progressive loading systematic review meta-analysis PubMed
Alfredson protocol Achilles tendinopathy eccentric loading
heavy slow resistance tendinopathy randomized controlled trial
collagen synthesis tendon running exercise PubMed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage across the tendon health spectrum: 2 meta-analyses, 2 systematic reviews, 4 RCTs, and 2 narrative reviews. Anchor meta-analyses from Bohm 2015 (tendon adaptation) and Maetz 2023 (Achilles loading protocols). Includes foundational Alfredson 1998 eccentric protocol, Beyer 2015 and Kongsgaard 2009 HSR trials, Silbernagel 2007 pain-monitoring RCT, and Kjaer 2009 collagen synthesis review. Most papers focus on Achilles or patellar tendon. Populations are predominantly recreational athletes and general adults; limited elite-specific data. Running-specific populations in Silbernagel 2007, Baxter 2021, and Alfredson 1998. Gap: no papers specifically on masters runners or age-related tendon changes.

papers.title: Human tendon adaptation in response to mechanical loading: a systematic review and meta-analysis of exercise intervention studies on healthy adults
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 27 studies examining in vivo tendon stiffness, Young's modulus, and cross-sectional area adaptations to chronic mechanical loading in healthy adults.

papers.title: Systematic Review and Meta-analyses of Randomized Controlled Trials Comparing Exercise Loading Protocols With Passive Treatment Modalities or Other Loading Protocols for the Management of Midportion Achilles Tendinopathy
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 12 RCTs comparing exercise loading protocols (eccentric, HSR, combined) with passive treatments and each other for midportion Achilles tendinopathy.

papers.title: Achilles and patellar tendinopathy loading programmes: a systematic review comparing clinical outcomes and identifying potential mechanisms for effectiveness
papers.notes: Systematic review of 32 studies comparing clinical outcomes of different loading programmes for Achilles and patellar tendinopathy and identifying potential mechanisms of effectiveness.

papers.title: Load progression criteria in exercise programmes in lower limb tendinopathy: a systematic review
papers.notes: Systematic review examining what load progression criteria are used in exercise programmes for lower limb tendinopathy and their evidence base.

papers.title: Heavy-load eccentric calf muscle training for the treatment of chronic Achilles tendinosis
papers.notes: Foundational study introducing the eccentric heel-drop protocol (180 reps/day for 12 weeks) for chronic midportion Achilles tendinosis in recreational athletes.

papers.title: Heavy Slow Resistance Versus Eccentric Training as Treatment for Achilles Tendinopathy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
papers.notes: RCT comparing 12-week heavy slow resistance training versus Alfredson eccentric protocol in patients with chronic midportion Achilles tendinopathy.

papers.title: Corticosteroid injections, eccentric decline squat training and heavy slow resistance training in patellar tendinopathy
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine and Science in Sports
papers.notes: Three-arm RCT comparing corticosteroid injections, eccentric decline squats, and heavy slow resistance training over 12 weeks in male patients with patellar tendinopathy.

papers.title: Continued sports activity, using a pain-monitoring model, during rehabilitation in patients with Achilles tendinopathy: a randomized controlled study
papers.notes: RCT investigating whether continued Achilles tendon-loading activity (running, jumping) using a pain-monitoring model during rehabilitation produces equivalent outcomes to activity cessation.

papers.title: From mechanical loading to collagen synthesis, structural changes and function in human tendon
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine and Science in Sports
papers.notes: Narrative review covering the pathway from mechanical loading to collagen synthesis, including growth factor signaling, gender differences, and structural adaptation in human tendon.

papers.title: The pathogenesis of tendinopathy: balancing the response to loading
papers.notes: Review examining how imbalance between tendon matrix degradation and synthesis in response to mechanical loading contributes to tendinopathy pathogenesis.

papers.title: Effectiveness of the Alfredson protocol compared with a lower repetition-volume protocol for midportion Achilles tendinopathy: a randomized controlled trial
papers.journal: Journal of Orthopaedic and Sports Physical Therapy
papers.notes: RCT comparing the standard Alfredson 180-rep/day eccentric protocol with a lower do-as-tolerated repetition volume protocol over 6 weeks for midportion Achilles tendinopathy.

papers.title: No Difference in Clinical Effects When Comparing Alfredson Eccentric and Silbernagel Combined Concentric-Eccentric Loading in Achilles Tendinopathy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
papers.notes: Multicenter RCT comparing Alfredson isolated eccentric versus Silbernagel combined concentric-eccentric loading programmes over 1 year in recreational athletes with midportion Achilles tendinopathy.

papers.title: Exercise Progression to Incrementally Load the Achilles Tendon
papers.journal: Medicine and Science in Sports and Exercise
papers.notes: Biomechanical study quantifying Achilles tendon loading across a progression of rehabilitation exercises from seated heel raises to single-leg hops, showing a 12-fold range in peak tendon loads.
